subject: Create cc-tasks task-patterns.md reference
description:
Create `claude/claude-tools/skills/cc-tasks/references/task-patterns.md` containing proven task design patterns for Claude Code Tasks.

Content must cover:

1. **Dependency Graph Design Patterns** — With diagrams/examples for each:
   - **Linear Chain**: A → B → C (sequential tasks)
   - **Fan-Out**: A → [B, C, D] (one task unblocks multiple parallel tasks)
   - **Fan-In**: [A, B, C] → D (multiple tasks must complete before one can start)
   - **Diamond**: A → [B, C] → D (fan-out then fan-in)

2. **Task Right-Sizing Guidance**:
   - Optimal granularity: 1-3 files per task
   - When to split: task touches >5 files, >2 hours estimated work, or crosses system boundaries
   - When to merge: tasks are trivially small (<10 lines each), always done together

3. **Multi-Agent Coordination Patterns**:
   - Self-claim workflow: agents call TaskList → find unblocked pending tasks → claim via TaskUpdate(owner)
   - Task-per-teammate ratio: aim for 2-3 tasks per agent per wave for balanced workload
   - Wave-based execution: tasks grouped by dependency level, all in one wave can run in parallel

4. **Metadata Strategies**:
   - Categorization: task_group, feature_name, source_section for filtering
   - Tracking: priority, complexity, task_uid for deduplication
   - Phase tracking: spec_phase, spec_phase_name for incremental execution
   - Custom metadata: produces_for for producer-consumer relationships

**Acceptance Criteria:**

_Functional:_
- [ ] Includes dependency graph design patterns (linear chain, fan-out, fan-in, diamond) with examples
- [ ] Task right-sizing guidance provided (optimal granularity: 1-3 files per task)
- [ ] Multi-agent coordination patterns documented (self-claim workflow, task-per-teammate ratio)
- [ ] Metadata strategies for categorization and tracking included
- [ ] Each pattern includes when-to-use guidance

_Edge Cases:_
- [ ] Patterns applicable to both single-agent and multi-agent scenarios
- [ ] File is self-contained and independently loadable

**Testing Requirements:**
• Verification: All four dependency graph patterns documented with examples
• Verification: Patterns are actionable with concrete tool call examples

Source: internal/specs/claude-tools-plugin-SPEC.md Section 5.1 (US-002)
